# Assembly Guide — so101_arm

*Auto-generated by botcad*

## Servo Bus Configuration

STS3215 servos are daisy-chained on a single UART half-duplex bus. Each servo needs a unique ID programmed via the Feetech debug board.

| Servo ID | Joint | Mode | Range |
|----------|-------|------|-------|
| 1 | shoulder_pan | Position | -110° to 110° |
| 2 | shoulder_lift | Position | -100° to 100° |
| 3 | elbow_flex | Position | -97° to 97° |
| 4 | wrist_flex | Position | -95° to 95° |
| 5 | wrist_roll | Position | -157° to 163° |
| 6 | gripper | Position | -10° to 100° |

## Servo Daisy Chain

```
  ID1(shoulder_pan) → ID2(shoulder_lift) → ID3(elbow_flex) → ID4(wrist_flex) → ID5(wrist_roll) → ID6(gripper)
```


## Wiring Diagram

```
Battery (LiPo 2S, 7.4V)
  ├── XT30 → Servo bus power (direct 7.4V)
  └── XT30 → 5V Buck converter → Raspberry Pi USB power

Raspberry Pi Zero 2W
  ├── GPIO 14 (TX) → Servo bus UART (via level shifter to 5V)
  └── CSI connector → Camera ribbon cable → OV5647
```

## Assembly Sequence

1. **Program servo IDs** — Connect each STS3215 individually to the Feetech debug board and assign IDs as listed above.

2. **Print structural parts** — Print body shells from the STL files in `meshes/`. Recommended: PLA or PETG, 0.2mm layer height, 20% infill.

3. **Assemble root body** (`base`):
   - Mount RaspberryPiZero2W (pi) at center position
   - Mount LiPo2S-1000 (battery) at bottom position

4. **Attach turntable** via STS3215 at joint `shoulder_pan`
   - Attach upper_arm via STS3215 at `shoulder_lift`

5. **Attach upper_arm** via STS3215 at joint `shoulder_lift`
   - Attach forearm via STS3215 at `elbow_flex`

6. **Attach forearm** via STS3215 at joint `elbow_flex`
   - Attach wrist via STS3215 at `wrist_flex`

7. **Attach wrist** via STS3215 at joint `wrist_flex`
   - Attach wrist_roll via STS3215 at `wrist_roll`

8. **Attach wrist_roll** via STS3215 at joint `wrist_roll`
   - Mount PiCamera2 (camera)
   - Attach jaw via STS3215 at `gripper`

9. **Attach jaw** via STS3215 at joint `gripper`

10. **Route servo bus cable** — Daisy-chain servos following the wiring diagram. Use the cable channel in each structural member.

11. **Connect camera** — Route CSI ribbon cable from OV5647 to Pi.

12. **Connect power** — Battery XT30 to servo bus power + buck converter → Pi USB.

13. **Test** — Power on, verify all servo IDs respond via `sts3215_scan.py`, test camera with `rpicam-hello`.
